title: orchis papilionacea var. cyrenaica
intro: Orchis papilionacea var. cyrenaica, commonly known as the lady's orchid, is a type of orchid native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique, showy flowers and long, thin leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat various urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and urinary retention. The health benefits of Orchis papilionacea var. cyrenaica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antibacterial agent, making it useful for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a powder or made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
